items.title: Coder – Delegate the coding to coder tasks powered by codex/Claude engines
items.why_it_matters: A new CLI tool called Coder lets you orchestrate your builds by delegating specific tasks from Claude Code to Codex or other cheaper engines. This matches your preferred flow of using Claude to direct while offloading heavy lifting to save tokens and costs. This week, install the CLI and delegate a simple component refactoring task in your AI-ingest repo to Codex.

items.title: Show HN: I used Claude to make some free fun pet-themed browser arcade games
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer used Claude to build and ship a collection of browser arcade games to teach kids about pet ownership. It is a direct reminder of how quickly you can go from an idea to a live URL when you let the AI do the heavy lifting. This week, pick the smallest pending feature on your AI-ingest roadmap, ask Claude to write it in one go, and deploy it to Vercel before you go to bed.

items.title: Claude Code weekly limits will be lower from Jul 13th
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic is lowering the weekly usage limits for Claude Code starting July 13th. Since this is your primary tool for writing code, you will need to be more deliberate with your prompts to avoid getting locked out mid-build. This week, try batching your feature requests into single, well-defined prompts rather than running multiple small, iterative terminal commands.

items.title: Show HN: Use GPT-5.6 and other models in Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: This open-source proxy lets you route Claude Code commands to other models like GPT-4o-mini or DeepSeek. It is a great way to bypass Anthropic's upcoming limit cuts by using cheaper models for basic boilerplate tasks. This week, set up the proxy on your laptop and run a simple CSS styling task using a cheaper model to save your Claude quota for complex logic.

items.title: Show HN: Aether – Run Claude Code, Codex, or OpenCode in devboxes you can watch
items.why_it_matters: Aether lets you run Claude Code and Codex inside cloud-hosted devboxes that you can monitor in real-time, preventing agent tasks from draining your laptop's RAM or battery. This is perfect for running longer generation tasks without freezing your local environment. This week, sign up for a free devbox and let Claude Code build out a new Supabase schema migration in the cloud.

items.title: Show HN: Confessor – replay what private info Claude Code accessed on your PC
items.why_it_matters: Confessor is a utility that lets you replay and audit exactly what private files and directories Claude Code accessed on your machine during a session. It gives you peace of mind when letting an AI agent run wild in your local workspace. This week, run Confessor after your next Claude Code session to verify exactly what parts of your Next.js project the agent analyzed.

items.title: Show HN: Token Time – Screen Time, but for your AI agent tokens
items.why_it_matters: Token Time acts like screen time but tracks your AI agent token usage and costs in real-time. It helps you keep an eye on your API spend before you get a surprise bill. This week, install the tool to monitor exactly how many tokens your AI-ingest development runs are consuming.
